title: -New Contract: New Contract: Tyler Technologies, Inc. for Open Data Portal (ODP) Hosting Services

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution authorizing the City Manager to execute a new contract with Tyler
Technologies, Inc., for the purposes of hosting the City’s Open Data Portal, in an amount
not to exceed $171,323 for the period commencing on July 1, 2024 through June 30,
2027.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
Funding for the hosting services for $171,323 is available in the Fiscal Year (FY) 2025-
2027 IT Cost Allocation Budget as itemized below, and funding for future years is subject
to Council’s approval of future budgets and Annual Appropriation Ordinances.
FY 2025: Fund 680 Software Maintenance
$54,345
(IT Cost Allocation, Software Renewal/Maintenance)
FY 2026: Fund 680 Software Maintenance
$57,062
(IT Cost Allocation, Software Renewal/Maintenance)
FY 2027: Fund 680 Software Maintenance
$59,916
(IT Cost Allocation, Software Renewal/Maintenance)
$171,323 Total FY 2025-2027 Software Maintenance
sections.current_situation:
The City of Berkeley’s Open Data Portal (https://data.cityofberkeley.info) is a web-based
presence established to serve as a single point of access for Open Data (non-confidential,
public data provided in searchable formats for unrestricted use to increase transparency
and civic engagement). The portal is loaded with datasets directly from city systems and
ready for download in numerous formats. The portal also provides a set of basic tools to
create charts, graphs, maps and other visualizations so visitors who do not want to
download the data can explore it in place.
Page 2 of 4
Public
New Contract: Tyler Technologies, Inc. For ODP CONSENT CALENDAR
April 29, 2025
The Department of Information Technology (IT) manages the City of Berkeley’s Open
Data Portal. Staff throughout the City, in coordination with IT and the City Manager’s
Office, upload city business data to the portal for public consumption. City information is
now easily accessible without requiring a Public Information Act request. The information
that was regularly requested is now online on the City of Berkeley Open Data Portal,
freeing staff to do other tasks.
The Open Data Portal aligns with Berkeley’s strategic goal to “Be a customer-focused
organization that provides excellent, timely, easily-accessible service and information to
the community”.
sections.background:
In August 2013, staff began researching and testing open data platforms to determine
which would most efficiently support a pilot program for the City of Berkeley in Fiscal Year
2015, given existing citywide fiscal and staff constraints. This included Open Data Portal
research, design, and documentation by interns of the Presidio Graduate School
(http://www.presidio.edu/).
In November 2013, Council issued a referral requesting the creation of an Open Data
Policy, citing the Federal Memorandum for Transparency and Open Government.
In June 2014, the Department of Information Technology executed a contract with
Socrata, Inc. (http://www.socrata.com/) to provide an Open Data platform and cloud
hosting. At the time, Socrata, Inc. was the only company whose primary focus was
providing open source data services to the public sector in the United States. Also in June
2014, the Department of Information Technology partnered with UC Berkeley’s School of
Information (http://www.ischool.berkeley.edu/) to identify and onboard a volunteer intern
Team Lead.
In November 2014, Staff provided an update to Council in City Council Information Report
#45. In December 2014, the Open Data Portal pilot project went live after more than
1,500 hours of combined staff, intern, and student volunteer work. The core objective of
the pilot program was to collect metrics around ODP performance, community use, and
feedback from both community members and staff. During this pilot program, the City
prioritized publishing datasets that community members frequently requested via Public
Records Act requests, resulting in 24/7 access to this data, improved service to our
community, and improved government transparency.
In 2018, Tyler Technologies, Inc. purchased the Socrata platform. Under Tyler
Technologies, Socrata has kept its name and branding. In 2019, staff assigned the
contract from Socrata, Inc. to Tyler Technologies, Inc. In 2025, a new contract was
created with Tyler Technologies, Inc., for the
sections.environmental:
By publishing the most frequently requested dataset requests, community members can
now access this information at home and electronically, thereby decreasing contributions
to global warming emissions and waste, respectively. This supports the Digital Strategic
Plan goal of providing more online services and the goal of reducing greenhouse gas
emissions of the City’s Climate Action Plan.
sections.rationale:
Tyler Technologies, Inc. offers the Socrata platform as a user friendly and cost-effective
solution for sharing the city’s business data across departments and with the public. This
contract will enable the Department of Information Technology to continue to offer this
valuable tool for an additional three years.
sections.alternatives:
Staff considered not renewing licenses for the Socrata platform, but doing so would
yield operation inefficiencies that would affect the quality of service delivered to City
staff and the community.
CONTACT PERSON
Kevin Fong, Director, Information Technology, 510-981-6525
sections.attachments:
1: Resolution
Page 4 of 4
Public
New Contract: Tyler Technologies, Inc. For ODP CONSENT CALENDAR
April 29, 2025
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
NEW CONTRACT: TYLER TECHNOLOGIES, INC. FOR OPEN DATA PORTAL (ODP)
HOSTING SERVICES
WHEREAS, the City of Berkeley’s Open Data Portal provides community members with
access to data that is non-confidential and public provided in searchable formats for
unrestricted use; and
WHEREAS, in August 2013, staff researched and tested multiple open data platforms
and in November 2013, Council issued a referral requesting the creation of an Open Data
Portal; and
WHEREAS, in June 2014, the Department of Information Technology executed a contract
with Socrata, Inc. to provide professional and hosting services for an Open Data Portal;
and in November 2014, Staff provided an update to Council with Information Report #45;
and in December 2014, the Open Data Portal went live; and
WHEREAS, more City information is now easily accessible without requiring a Public
Information Act request; and
WHEREAS, government transparency is improved because the portal provides members
of the public with the same access to raw data that governments have; and
WHEREAS, in 2018, Tyler Technologies, Inc. purchased the Socrata platform and, in
2019, staff assigned the contract from Socrata, Inc. to Tyler Technologies, Inc. and in
2025 executed a new contract with Tyler Technologies, Inc. that supercedes the Socrata
contract, but assumes the same terms and conditions; and
WHEREAS, funding for the additional hosting services for $171,323 is available in the
Fiscal Year (FY) 2025-2027 IT Cost Allocation Budget, and funding for future years is
subject to Council’s approval of the proposed citywide Fiscal Year FY 2025-2027 Budget
and Annual Appropriation Ordinances.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the
City Manager is authorized to execute a new contract with Tyler Technologies, Inc., for
the purposes of hosting the City’s Open Data P
